name: ◁ GRILLED BANANAS WRAPPED IN STICKY RICE

ingredients.item:
I used to ride my bike home from school, and whenever I passed a street vendor selling chuối bọc nếp nướng, I simply couldn’t resist the tempting aroma—neither will you.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD4 servings

BANANAS AND STICKY RICE
4 ripe Asian dwarf bananas, peeled
2 tablespoons sugar, divided

1 cup (200 g) sticky rice, rinsed, soaked for 1 hour, and drained
Pinch of salt
¼ cup (60 ml) water
¼ cup (60 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk
2 pandan leaves, knotted into a set, or 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
Vegetable oil, for greasing hands (optional)
ingredients.original:
I used to ride my bike home from school, and whenever I passed a street vendor selling chuối bọc nếp nướng, I simply couldn’t resist the tempting aroma—neither will you.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD4 servings

BANANAS AND STICKY RICE
4 ripe Asian dwarf bananas, peeled
2 tablespoons sugar, divided

1 cup (200 g) sticky rice, rinsed, soaked for 1 hour, and drained
Pinch of salt
¼ cup (60 ml) water
¼ cup (60 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk
2 pandan leaves, knotted into a set, or 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
Vegetable oil, for greasing hands (optional)

ingredients.item: 4 pieces (2 by 6 inches/5 by 15 cm) and 4 pieces (3 by 6 inches/7.5 by 15 cm) banana leaves, prepped
ingredients.original: 4 pieces (2 by 6 inches/5 by 15 cm) and 4 pieces (3 by 6 inches/7.5 by 15 cm) banana leaves, prepped

ingredients.item:
SAUCE
1 cup (240 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk
1 tablespoon sugar
ingredients.original:
SAUCE
1 cup (240 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk
1 tablespoon sugar

ingredients.item: 2 pandan leaves, knotted into a set, or 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
ingredients.original: 2 pandan leaves, knotted into a set, or 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

ingredients.item: 3 tablespoons crushed roasted peanuts, for garnish (optional)
ingredients.original: 3 tablespoons crushed roasted peanuts, for garnish (optional)

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE BANANAS AND STICKY RICE Place bananas in a medium bowl and toss with 1 tablespoon of the sugar. Cover and refrigerate for about 30 minutes.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE BANANAS AND STICKY RICE Place bananas in a medium bowl and toss with 1 tablespoon of the sugar. Cover and refrigerate for about 30 minutes.

ingredients.item: 2. Toss the sticky rice with the pinch of salt, then add to a small saucepan with a tight-fitting lid with the water and ¼ cup (60 ml) of coconut milk. Add 1 set of the knotted pandan leaves and bring to a boil. Cook uncovered over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until water level drops below the surface of the rice (about 5 minutes). Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for an additional 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let sit for 5 minutes.
ingredients.original: 2. Toss the sticky rice with the pinch of salt, then add to a small saucepan with a tight-fitting lid with the water and ¼ cup (60 ml) of coconut milk. Add 1 set of the knotted pandan leaves and bring to a boil. Cook uncovered over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until water level drops below the surface of the rice (about 5 minutes). Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for an additional 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let sit for 5 minutes.

ingredients.item: 3. Remove and discard pandan leaves, add the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar and fluff with a fork or pair of chopsticks. While rice is still warm, wear gloves or grease your hands and pinch one-quarter of the rice and squeeze to compress it into a single solid mass. Spread a 12-inch (30 cm) square piece of plastic wrap on a flat surface and place the sticky rice mass on it, slightly off-center. Fold the plastic wrap in half to cover the rice. Use a rolling pin to flatten it into a ⅔-inch (1.7 cm) thick oval disc.
ingredients.original: 3. Remove and discard pandan leaves, add the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar and fluff with a fork or pair of chopsticks. While rice is still warm, wear gloves or grease your hands and pinch one-quarter of the rice and squeeze to compress it into a single solid mass. Spread a 12-inch (30 cm) square piece of plastic wrap on a flat surface and place the sticky rice mass on it, slightly off-center. Fold the plastic wrap in half to cover the rice. Use a rolling pin to flatten it into a ⅔-inch (1.7 cm) thick oval disc.

ingredients.item: 4. Unfold the plastic wrap and place a banana in the center on top of the rice. With the plastic wrap, fold the rice over to fully cover the banana, making sure the rice encloses and adheres to it tightly. Twist the ends of the plastic wrap tightly like a piece of taffy. Remove the plastic wrap and use a smaller piece of banana leaf to wrap around the banana lengthwise and a larger piece to wrap around the width. Repeat with the remaining bananas, sticky rice, and banana leaves.
ingredients.original: 4. Unfold the plastic wrap and place a banana in the center on top of the rice. With the plastic wrap, fold the rice over to fully cover the banana, making sure the rice encloses and adheres to it tightly. Twist the ends of the plastic wrap tightly like a piece of taffy. Remove the plastic wrap and use a smaller piece of banana leaf to wrap around the banana lengthwise and a larger piece to wrap around the width. Repeat with the remaining bananas, sticky rice, and banana leaves.

ingredients.item: 5. Grill the four parcels, uncovered, on a charcoal or gas grill (uncovered) or in an infrared stove at 480°F (250°C), rotating occasionally. When the banana leaves are scorched and the surface of the sticky rice is dry to the touch (after about 10 minutes), you can remove both leaves. Continue to grill, rotating occasionally, until a crispy golden crust forms (about 15 more minutes).
ingredients.original: 5. Grill the four parcels, uncovered, on a charcoal or gas grill (uncovered) or in an infrared stove at 480°F (250°C), rotating occasionally. When the banana leaves are scorched and the surface of the sticky rice is dry to the touch (after about 10 minutes), you can remove both leaves. Continue to grill, rotating occasionally, until a crispy golden crust forms (about 15 more minutes).

ingredients.item: 6. MAKE THE SAUCE In a small saucepan, combine 1 cup (240 ml) of coconut milk, 1 tablespoon of sugar, tapioca starch, and knotted pandan leaves, and whisk well. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, whisking constantly, until slightly thickened (about 3 minutes). Remove and discard pandan leaves.
ingredients.original: 6. MAKE THE SAUCE In a small saucepan, combine 1 cup (240 ml) of coconut milk, 1 tablespoon of sugar, tapioca starch, and knotted pandan leaves, and whisk well. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, whisking constantly, until slightly thickened (about 3 minutes). Remove and discard pandan leaves.

ingredients.item: 7. Cut rolls into bite-size pieces, drizzle with sauce, sprinkle on crushed roasted peanuts (if using), and serve hot.
ingredients.original: 7. Cut rolls into bite-size pieces, drizzle with sauce, sprinkle on crushed roasted peanuts (if using), and serve hot.
